description_long: This is a vintage, rectangular toaster with a robust, dark metallic exterior and rounded edges, giving it a sturdy and industrial appearance. The surface is mostly smooth with some visible wear and minor scuff marks, suggesting frequent use. On one side, there is a prominent lever mechanism, likely for lowering bread into the toaster, and a small, circular component that may serve as a button or indicator. The front face features a vented grille and a small window or slot, possibly for monitoring the toasting process. Subtle metallic accents and a faintly visible brand name add to its retro aesthetic. The overall build appears solid, with functional parts integrated into the design for practical use in an urban kitchen or garage setting.
description: A vintage, dark metallic toaster with a robust build, lever mechanism, and vented front grille, showing signs of wear and practical use.
description_view_0: The top and side of the toaster are visible, highlighting the smooth, dark surface and the lever mechanism on the right.
description_view_1: This angled view emphasizes the length of the toaster and the lever, with the side grille and a small circular component visible.
description_view_2: The side view shows the lever, a roller or knob, and the vented front grille, with more detail on the functional parts.
description_view_3: The front and top are visible, showing the vented grille, a small window or slot, and the overall compact shape.
attribute_brand_logo: A faint, small brand name is visible on the front grille, but the text is not clearly readable.
attribute_control_knobs: One roller or knob is visible on the side, dark in color, with no clear markings or labels.
attribute_power_cord: A short, dark power cord is visible on the left side, with a plug attached.
attribute_toast_slots: No visible toast slots are present on the exterior surfaces shown.
attribute_crumb_tray: No crumb tray is visible on any side of the toaster.
